title: BBS Movie Studio
description: Simple Modpack built around the blessing of a mod created by McHorse called "BBS Mod"
body:
# BBS Movie Studio

Once called Emil's BBS Filmstudio, this modpack has been built from the ground up in it' 2.0 version and is now a bit simpler with only 42 instead of 67 mods, it should run smooth on most systems. This new version comes with a selection of shaders for you to try. I think you should give [Photon](https://modrinth.com/shader/photon-shader), [Nostalgia](https://modrinth.com/shader/nostalgia-shader) and [Bliss](https://modrinth.com/shader/bliss-shader) a try.

Information on [Walk, Jog, Run](https://modrinth.com/mod/walk-jog-run) Mod, you can toggle between walking and jogging with Left Alt, and I set the walk speed so that it matches the animated footsteps of the player models that come with BBS, so when recording, consider switching to Walk Mode.

I figured, when you make movie, you might need to build some big sets and/or landscapes, so [Axiom](https://modrinth.com/mod/axiom) comes with this modpack, if you are not familiar with [Axiom](https://modrinth.com/mod/axiom) you can either remove it or check out it's [documentation](https://axiomdocs.moulberry.com/).

If you don't know what [BBS(Blockbuster Studio)](https://modrinth.com/mod/bbs-mod) by McHorse is, it is a newer version of his 8-year old [Blockbuster](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/blockbuster) Mod, these mods allow you to create films in Minecraft, similar to ReplayMod or Flashback, with the difference, that you can import models from Blockbench, and you have to record all actions yourself. A very important perk is, that you can adjust every single motion the way you want, this allows you to basically create anything you want within minecraft.

If you are willing to make some movies in the game, this is pack is my suggestion, if you need help with BBS visit [McHorse's Youtube Channel](https://www.youtube.com/@McHorsesCreations), you'll find loads of tutorials and walk throughs.

MC Version: 1.20.4, 1.21.1 (soon)

This description was last changed on October 11
